Description: Path to eval_results.json from evaluation step — must contain a "metrics" dict
Task type — determines default metric/direction and fallback strategy dispatch. Custom values supported when task_description is provided.
Free-text description of the task and goals — included in LLM prompt for domain context (e.g., "Fine-tuning a food classifier for mobile with latency constraints")
Metric name for threshold comparison — 'auto' resolves per task_type (detection: AP, classification: accuracy/top1, llm_finetune: eval_loss)
Whether higher or lower metric is better — auto-resolves from task_type or metric name patterns (loss→minimize, accuracy→maximize)
Quality gate — metric must meet this to trigger deploy (detection: 0.50, classification: 0.85, LLM: 1.5)
Current loop iteration counter (1-indexed) — included in prompt for budget awareness
Maximum training iterations before stop (0 = deploy-or-stop gate only). LLM uses this to gauge exploration budget.
JSON array of all previous iteration records — the FULL history is passed to the LLM for trend analysis. Each entry: {iteration, hyperparams, metrics, decision, reason, reasoning, method}
Current iteration's hyperparameters — shown to LLM as the baseline to adjust

Available HP ranges for the LLM to choose from. Auto resolves task-specific defaults. Grid format: {"param": [v1,v2]}. Random format: {"param": {"type": "log_uniform", "low": 1e-5, "high": 1e-2}}. LLM sees bounds and must stay within them.
HP adjustment strategy used by fallback only (LLM chooses its own approach). Determines fallback behavior when LLM is unavailable.
Fallback schedule strategy: multiply LR by this factor each iteration
Fallback schedule strategy: reduce frozen_stages by 1 for vision detection tasks
Fallback early stop: minimum improvement between consecutive iterations. 0 disables. LLM does its own trend analysis instead.
Fallback overfitting detection: check train_loss vs eval_loss divergence. LLM assesses overfitting from full metric history instead.
Clarifai model URL for LLM inference (e.g., https://clarifai.com/openai/chat-completion/models/gpt-oss-120b). Empty string = use fallback only (no LLM).
LLM sampling temperature — lower values (0.0–0.2) produce more deterministic decisions. Higher values increase exploration in HP suggestions.
Maximum retry attempts when LLM returns invalid/unparseable output. After exhausting retries the step falls back to hardcoded logic.
Random seed for fallback grid/random strategies — uses (seed + iteration) for deterministic reproducibility
Routing decision for Argo conditional branching — produced by LLM or fallback
Next hyperparameter set for retraining (only meaningful when decision=retrain). Produced by LLM with validation+clamping, or by fallback strategy.
LLM explanation of the decision (2–4 sentences). Includes trend analysis, tradeoff assessment. Prefixed with [FALLBACK] when using fallback path.
Current primary metric value (formatted to 6 decimal places)
Resolved primary metric name (e.g. AP or accuracy/top1 or eval_loss)
Updated history with current iteration appended — includes reasoning and method fields for observability
Whether overfitting was detected — assessed by LLM from metric trends, or by fallback from train/eval loss ratio
Passthrough of current iteration for downstream reference
Incremented iteration counter for recursive retrain call
Debug metadata: {method: llm|fallback, model_url, temperature, attempts, latency_ms, prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, fallback_reason, ...}
